title: Ipomoea thailandica
taxonomy: Ipomoea thailandica is a species of flowering plant in the family Convolvulaceae. It is a member of the genus Ipomoea and is native to Thailand. The species is a type of morning glory, characterized by its distinctive flowers and climbing habit. The classification of Ipomoea thailandica is as a species of flowering plant within the family of bindweeds and morning glories. This classification places it within the larger group of flowering plants known as angiosperms.

intro: Ipomoea thailandica, also known as Thai morning glory, is a species of flowering vine in the Convolvulaceae family. This plant has been traditionally used in Thai medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and skin irritations, due to its reported health benefits, such as anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Ipomoea thailandica include alkaloids, flavonoids, and glycosides, which are thought to contribute to its medicinal effects. The most common herbal preparations made with Ipomoea thailandica include teas, infusions, and capsules, although its use as a medicinal herb requires further scientific research to confirm its safety and efficacy.
